answer:
There is no clinical guideline or evidence supporting the use of semaglutide to treat Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). The search results yielded no relevant information. 

 Semaglutide is an FDA-approved medication for type 2 diabetes, and it has been investigated for other indications, but there is currently no established role for semaglutide in the treatment of ASD.
